hero.title: Education
hero.intro: Discover courses, guides, and educational resources to deepen your understanding of industrial hemp, regenerative agriculture, and the sustainability transition.

sections.title: Industrial Hemp Fundamentals
sections.subtitle: Courses, guides, and readings on hemp biology, uses, and cultivation basics.

cards.title: Introduction to Industrial Hemp: Biology, Uses & Potential
cards.excerpt: A beginner-level overview of Cannabis sativa L., covering plant biology, primary applications (fibre, hurd, seed), and global market trends.

cards.title: Understanding THC vs. CBD: What Makes Hemp Industrial?
cards.excerpt: A plain-language explainer on the chemical distinction between industrial hemp and cannabis, and why the 0.2% THC threshold matters in regulation.

cards.title: Hemp Across History: From Ancient Fibre Crop to Modern Biotech
cards.excerpt: A recorded lecture tracing industrial hemp's agrarian history and its resurgence as a sustainable material in the 21st century.

cards.title: The Anatomy of a Hemp Plant: A Field Guide
cards.excerpt: Illustrated breakdown of hemp plant anatomy — from root system to bract — with notes on which parts yield which commercial outputs.

cards.title: Hemp Cultivation 101: Growing Conditions & Crop Management
cards.excerpt: Foundational course covering soil requirements, water needs, seeding density, and harvesting windows for industrial hemp.

sections.title: Sustainability & Climate
sections.subtitle: Resources on carbon capture, regenerative agriculture, and the Saudi Green Initiative.

cards.title: Carbon Sequestration Basics: How Plants Capture CO₂
cards.excerpt: Introduction to the mechanisms of biological carbon capture, with a focus on fast-growing crops and their role in climate mitigation strategies.

cards.title: Regenerative Agriculture: Principles and Practice
cards.excerpt: Overview of soil regeneration techniques including cover cropping, minimal tillage, and composting, applied to arid-zone farming contexts.

cards.title: Understanding Life Cycle Assessment for Bio-Based Materials
cards.excerpt: Step-by-step walkthrough of LCA methodology, aimed at professionals evaluating sustainable material choices for construction and industry.

cards.title: Saudi Vision 2030 & the Green Initiative: A Primer
cards.excerpt: Summary of Saudi Arabia's key environmental commitments — the Saudi Green Initiative, the Middle East Green Initiative, and their targets for land restoration and emissions reduction.

cards.title: Water Stewardship in Arid Agriculture
cards.excerpt: Practical course on water-efficient irrigation strategies, wastewater reuse, and drought-resilient crop selection for farming in low-rainfall environments.

sections.title: Biotechnology & Innovation
sections.subtitle: Biotech concepts, bio-based materials, and circular economy explainers.

cards.title: Introduction to Biotechnology: From Lab to Field
cards.excerpt: Overview of core biotech concepts — fermentation, tissue culture, genetic markers — explained for non-specialist audiences in the agri-environmental sector.

cards.title: Bio-Based Materials: Replacing Plastics and Concrete
cards.excerpt: Recorded seminar on the science and market readiness of bio-composites, hempcrete, and bioplastics as viable industrial material substitutes.

cards.title: What Is a Biorefinery? A Non-Technical Explainer
cards.excerpt: Plain-language introduction to the biorefinery model — how crop biomass is fractionated into multiple valuable outputs with near-zero waste.

cards.title: Circular Economy Principles: A Starter Reading List
cards.excerpt: Curated selection of accessible articles and reports introducing circular design, closed-loop systems, and waste valorisation concepts.

cards.title: Plant Genetics & Crop Improvement for Beginners
cards.excerpt: Accessible course covering selective breeding, marker-assisted selection, and how these techniques are applied to improve industrial hemp varieties.
